name: Backend Engineering
description: APIs, databases, distributed systems, and microservices
axioms: CAP theorem: distributed system can have at most 2 of Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance
ACID: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability for transactions
REST: stateless, resource-based, standard HTTP methods, hypermedia links
Database normalization reduces redundancy; denormalization improves read performance
Every dependency is a potential failure point
APIs are contracts: design for backward compatibility and versioning
reasoning_patterns: API design: resource modeling, endpoint naming, versioning strategy
Database schema design: normalize for writes, denormalize for reads
Caching strategy: cache-aside, write-through, write-behind (trade consistency for speed)
Circuit breaker: fail fast when dependency is down, recover gracefully
Event sourcing: store state changes as immutable events, derive current state
boundary_conditions: Does not cover specific databases (Postgres, MongoDB) internals in depth
Does not cover service mesh (Istio, Linkerd) in detail
GraphQL has different tradeoffs than REST
key_references: Bernstein, P. Principles of Transaction Processing
Fowler, M. Patterns of Enterprise Application Architecture
canonical_truths: Network calls can fail. Always design for timeout, retry, and fallback.
Database indexes speed up reads but slow down writes and consume storage
Microservices trade complexity for independent deployability
Idempotency keys prevent duplicate processing in unreliable networks
Connection pooling is essential for database performance at scale
